Change Name: Rebuild hero around one clear service promise
Recommendation: Make the homepage hero instantly explain that UtahLister creates and posts sharper Marketplace and KSL listings for sellers who want stronger results without doing the work
Decision Notes: Backed by the homepage research brief and trust-first UX research

Recommendation: Test a primary CTA that directly repeats the hero promise instead of introducing a second concept
Decision Notes: Current best candidate is a direct service CTA such as Get My Listing Done or a close equivalent that matches the final hero copy

Change Name: Show specific proof in the first two sections
Recommendation: Move real proof stories and concrete outcomes earlier and keep them specific
Decision Notes: Trek and Dr. Pen style proof should appear early instead of vague optimization claims

Change Name: Adopt seller-owned posting as the default operating model
Recommendation: UtahLister should prepare the listing package and support posting while the seller posts from their own accounts
Decision Notes: This reduces credential risk trust mismatch and pickup logistics problems

Change Name: Prohibit password collection in the standard workflow
Recommendation: Do not ask for or store customer passwords as the default service model
Decision Notes: If any exception is ever considered it should require a separate risk review and written process

Recommendation: Write homepage and intake copy in short direct customer-centered language
Decision Notes: Supports comprehension and trust for a broad audience

Change Name: Keep persuasion high and deception low
Recommendation: Avoid fake scarcity confusing CTA wording and other dark-pattern style tactics
Decision Notes: Long-term trust and defensibility matter more than short-term manipulation
